methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $4.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $810M - $2.4B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Ultima Genomics, a Series C biotech company founded by Gilad Almogy, carries a current post-money valuation of $4.0B after raising $700M+. The peer universe consists of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage peers in the Biotech & Health sector, with same-stage privates including Insitro ($2.4B), Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B), and New Limit ($810M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $4.0B last post-money, implying $6.8B. Peer-median anchoring across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $810M to $2.4B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; while the Biotech & Health public universe trades at a 6.1x price-to-sales median, Ultima's revenue is not disclosed, precluding this method.

The triangulated analysis across applicable methods supports a valuation range of $1.3B (low) to $7.8B (high), with a median of $4.2B. This median aligns with the current $4.0B valuation. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion between peer-based ($1.5B) and stage-progression ($6.8B) results, reflecting heterogeneity in biotech financing and the absence of revenue data to anchor public comparables.
